name: Qdrant Vector Database Embedding Pipeline
description: Automated workflow: Qdrant Vector Database Embedding Pipeline. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, vectorStoreQdrant, ftp, documentDefaultDataLoader, stopAndError. It contains 14 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Qdrant Vector Database Embedding Pipeline workflow containing 14 nodes.

parameters.content:
### Fetch JSON File List
**Node:** FTP (all files)
**Operation:** List
**Path:** <file path>

Recursively lists all .json files prepared for embedding.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### Iterate Over Files
**Node:** Loop Over Items

Batches each file path individually for processing.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### Download Each File
**Node:** FTP (1 file download)

Downloads the current file in binary form using:
```
Path = file_path/{{ $json.name }}
```

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### Parse JSON Document (Default Data Loader)
**Node:** Default Data Loader
**Loader Type**: binary
- Converts JSON structure into a document format compatible with embedding.


### Split into Smaller Chunks
**Node:** Character Text Splitter
**Split by:** "chunk_id" or custom logic based on chunk formatting

Optional node if chunk size normalization is required before embedding.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
### Store in Vector DB
**Node:** Qdrant Vector Store
**Batch Size:** 100

**Collection:** <collection_name>
Sends cleaned text chunks to OpenAI to get embeddings (1536 dim for text-embedding-ada-002)

#### collection settings in Qdrant cluster
```
PUT /collections/{collection_name}
{
    "vectors": {
      "size": 1536,
      "distance": "Cosine"
    }
}
```
Embed Chunks
**Node:** Embeddings OpenAI
Pushes the embedded chunks (with metadata) into Qdrant for semantic retrieval.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Qdrant Vector Database Embedding Pipeline. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, vectorStoreQdrant, ftp, documentDefaultDataLoader, stopAndError. It contains 14 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Qdrant Vector Database Embedding Pipeline. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.content:
### Fetch JSON File List
**Node:** FTP (all files)
**Operation:** List
**Path:** <file path>

Recursively lists all .json files prepared for embedding.

### Iterate Over Files
**Node:** Loop Over Items

Batches each file path individually for processing.

### Download Each File
**Node:** FTP (1 file download)

Downloads the current file in binary form using:
```
Path = file_path/{{ $json.name }}
```

### Parse JSON Document (Default Data Loader)
**Node:** Default Data Loader
**Loader Type**: binary
- Converts JSON structure into a document format compatible with embedding.


### Split into Smaller Chunks
**Node:** Character Text Splitter
**Split by:** "chunk_id" or custom logic based on chunk formatting

Optional node if chunk size normalization is required before embedding.

### Store in Vector DB
**Node:** Qdrant Vector Store
**Batch Size:** 100

**Collection:** <collection_name>
Sends cleaned text chunks to OpenAI to get embeddings (1536 dim for text-embedding-ada-002)

#### collection settings in Qdrant cluster
```
PUT /collections/{collection_name}
{
    "vectors": {
      "size": 1536,
      "distance": "Cosine"
    }
}
```
Embed Chunks
**Node:** Embeddings OpenAI
Pushes the embedded chunks (with metadata) into Qdrant for semantic retrieval.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Qdrant Vector Store" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Default Data Loader" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Character Text Splitter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "List all the files" node (n8n-nodes-base.ftp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop over one item" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Downloading item" node (n8n-nodes-base.ftp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
